task_completion.description: Plot vertices A and B at the correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Points A and B are visible on the GeoGebra coordinate plane at exactly (0, 0) and (4, 0).

task_completion.description: Plot vertex C at the correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Point C is visible on the GeoGebra coordinate plane at approximately (2, 3.46), forming an equilateral triangle with A and B.

task_completion.description: Construct three circular arcs to form the Reuleaux triangle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three outward-curving circular arcs are drawn. Each arc connects two of the vertices (A-B, B-C, C-A) and is centered at the third vertex, forming the characteristic curved Reuleaux triangle shape.

task_completion.description: Include a text annotation with the phrase 'constant width'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box or label is visible in the GeoGebra view containing the exact words 'constant width'.
The GeoGebra window title bar shows 'reuleaux_triangle' or a save dialog is visible confirming the file is being saved as 'reuleaux_triangle.ggb' in the ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GeoGebra graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoGebra application open and being actively used (e.g., toolbar selections, algebra view updates), rather than terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent used appropriate geometric tools rather than freehand drawing or importing an image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view on the left side of the GeoGebra window shows mathematical definitions for points and arcs (e.g., 'CircularArc' or 'Arc'), not image objects or freehand strokes.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by manually writing the .ggb archive contents.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are shown editing geogebra.xml or other internal files of the .ggb format.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Vertices must be exactly A=(0,0), B=(4,0), and C=(2, 3.4641). The boundary must consist of exactly 3 circular arcs, each connecting two vertices and centered at the third. A text annotation with the exact phrase 'constant width' must be visible. The expected filename is 'reuleaux_triangle.ggb'.
